Title: The regulatory module MdCPCL-MdILR3L mediates the synthesis of ascorbic acid and anthocyanin in apple

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrid offspring of Xinjiang red-fleshed apple ('Zihong No. 1' × 'Gala'); 'Orin' callus; 'Gala' fruit
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit flesh/pulp; callus in transgenic assays
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: D-mannose/L-galactose pathway of ascorbic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms immediate precursor to the target product and is a key determinant of target-product accumulation in fruit pulp
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point for AsA accumulation in apple pulp in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher MdGLDH expression is strongly positively associated with increased ascorbic acid accumulation; its transcription is activated by MdCPCL and further enhanced by the MdCPCL-MdILR3L complex
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among surveyed D-Man/L-Gal pathway genes, MdGLDH expression showed the strongest correlation with AsA content during fruit development and across hybrid offspring. 2. Red-fleshed apple pulp had significantly higher MdGLDH expression and AsA than non-red-fleshed pulp. 3. MdCPCL directly bound the MdGLDH promoter and activated transcription by Y1H, EMSA, LUC and ChIP assays. 4. MdCPCL overexpression increased MdGLDH transcript and AsA content in callus and fruit, while MdCPCL knockdown/silencing reduced both. 5. Co-expression with MdILR3L further enhanced MdGLDH expression and AsA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Developmental correlation analysis; RT-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase reporter assay; ChIP-qPCR; transgenic overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockdown; transient fruit overexpression/silencing; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in red-fleshed apple pulp relative to non-red-fleshed pulp; expression tracks AsA content during fruit development; elevated in MdCPCL-overexpression and MdCPCL+MdILR3L callus
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native MdGLDH promoter in reporter assays; CaMV 35S for upstream regulator overexpression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit development stage; light treatment after dark culture in callus; transient infiltration in fruit followed by dark then constant light incubation
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MdCPCL; MdILR3L (indirectly enhances promoter binding via complex with MdCPCL)
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FAD-dependent dehydrogenase family (inferred from enzyme identity)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study argues MdGLDH is especially important for AsA synthesis in apple fruit pulp; discussion notes possible organ-specific differences between fruit and leaves.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to red flesh coloration and dietary flavonoid nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit flesh/pulp; callus in transgenic assays
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate anthocyanidin precursors for anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins in the late anthocyanin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting, but identified as a primary structural target activated by the MdCPCL-MdILR3L module
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activation of MdANS expression promotes anthocyanin accumulation; MdCPCL directly activates its promoter and the MdCPCL-MdILR3L complex further enhances transcription
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdANS expression increased strongly in MdCPCL-overexpression callus and fruit and decreased after MdCPCL knockdown/silencing. 2. MdCPCL directly bound the MdANS promoter but not the MdUFGT promoter. 3. Promoter activation of MdANS by MdCPCL was confirmed by Y1H, EMSA, LUC and ChIP assays. 4. Co-expression of MdCPCL with MdILR3L further increased MdANS transcription and anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; dual-luciferase reporter assay; ChIP-qPCR; transgenic overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockdown; transient fruit overexpression/silencing; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in MdCPCL-overexpression callus and fruit; downregulated in MdCPCL knockdown/silenced material; further increased in MdCPCL+MdILR3L callus
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native MdANS promoter in reporter assays; CaMV 35S for upstream regulator overexpression constructs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light treatment after dark culture in callus; transient infiltration in fruit followed by dark then constant light incubation
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MdCPCL; MdILR3L (indirectly via complex with MdCPCL)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MdUFGT was also transcriptionally responsive to MdCPCL perturbation, but direct binding was not detected; MdANS is the demonstrated direct anthocyanin structural target.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R3-MYB family; contains a conserved SANT domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ascorbic acid is an essential micronutrient for human health; anthocyanin contributes dietary flavonoids and red flesh coloration
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit flesh/pulp; callus; infiltrated fruit tissue
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central positive regulator directly connecting AsA and anthocyanin biosynthesis by activating one key structural gene in each pathway
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases ascorbic acid and anthocyanin accumulation, whereas knockdown/silencing decreases both
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCPCL was identified by Y1H screening using the MdGLDH promoter as bait. 2. MdCPCL localizes to the nucleus and directly binds the promoters of MdGLDH and MdANS. 3. MdCPCL activates transcription of MdGLDH and MdANS in LUC assays and in vivo ChIP assays. 4. Stable overexpression in callus and transient overexpression in fruit increased AsA and anthocyanin content, while CRISPR/Cas9 knockdown or VIGS reduced both. 5. MdCPCL interacts with MdILR3L, and this complex further enhances downstream gene activation and metabolite accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for biofortification of apple flesh with both vitamin C and anthocyanin; may be useful alone or stacked with MdILR3L, while monitoring broader effects on flavonoid and AsA pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of ascorbic acid biosynthesis and anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates MdGLDH and MdANS; also increases expression of MdUFGT, MdGGP and MdGDH, though direct regulation was only demonstrated for MdGLDH and MdANS
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H screening; EMSA; dual-luciferase reporter assay; ChIP-qPCR; stable overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockdown; transient overexpression and silencing in fruit; RT-qPCR; metabolite quantification
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for stable overexpression constructs; pHB-GFP and pTRV2 for transient fruit assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation by promoter binding to MBS cis-elements
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage comparisons; light induction after dark culture in callus; dark then constant light after fruit infiltration
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Did not directly bind the MdUFGT promoter. Discussion suggests MdCPCL may also directly regulate MdGGP, but this was not demonstrated in the main results.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a heterodimer/complex with MdILR3L that enhances DNA-binding and transcriptional activation

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH family; contains a conserved HLH domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ascorbic acid is an essential micronutrient for human health; anthocyanin contributes dietary flavonoids and red flesh coloration
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: callus; inferred fruit flesh/pulp in the regulatory model
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Co-regulator that strengthens MdCPCL-dependent activation of both AsA and anthocyanin pathways
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: When co-expressed with MdCPCL, further increases ascorbic acid and anthocyanin accumulation beyond MdCPCL alone
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdILR3L was identified as an MdCPCL-interacting bHLH factor by Y2H screening. 2. Interaction with MdCPCL was confirmed by Y2H, pull-down, LCI and BiFC assays. 3. Co-overexpression with MdCPCL in 'Orin' callus increased AsA and anthocyanin levels and elevated MdGLDH and MdANS expression more than MdCPCL alone. 4. EMSA and LUC assays showed MdILR3L enhances the binding and transcriptional activation capacity of MdCPCL on MdGLDH and MdANS promoters. 5. MdILR3L may also bind the MdCPCL promoter E-boxes, but it did not directly bind MdGLDH or MdANS promoters in Y1H assays.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a stacking partner with MdCPCL to further elevate vitamin C and anthocyanin in apple biofortification strategies
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cooperative transcriptional activation of ascorbic acid and anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances MdCPCL-mediated activation of MdGLDH and MdANS; may promote MdCPCL transcription by binding E-boxes in the MdCPCL promoter
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible feedforward regulation if MdILR3L activates MdCPCL transcription, but not fully established
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; pull-down assay; LCI; BiFC; co-overexpression in callus; EMSA; dual-luciferase reporter assay; RT-qPCR; metabolite quantification
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MdCPCL (putative direct target); MdGLDH and MdANS indirectly through complex with MdCPCL
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein–protein interaction-mediated enhancement of DNA binding and transcriptional activation; possible direct transcriptional activation of MdCPCL
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light culture conditions after dark growth in callus
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper explicitly states MdILR3L cannot directly bind MdGLDH or MdANS promoters in Y1H assays, so its effects on these genes are mediated through MdCPCL or possibly via MdCPCL transcriptional activation.
